query: Voyager 1 distance from Earth light-day milestone date 2026
a_issues: Contains some lower-quality/noisy results such as Reddit and Facebook, and one SpaceDaily result gives only a generic 'in November 2026' phrasing. But A includes multiple corroborating results, including EarthSky and IFLScience, stating November 18, 2026, with no major internal contradiction in the top answer.
b_issues: Includes contradictory dates. The featured snippet and top IFLScience result say November 18, 2026, but other results say November 15, 2026 (Popular Science) and November 13, 2026 (older IFLScience article). The top result text itself also oddly says 'On November 15, Voyager 1 will reach 25.9 billion kilometers...' while the title says November 18, creating internal inconsistency.
reasoning: A better enables a correct answer because it prominently contains the anchor-consistent date: Voyager 1 reaches the 1 light-day milestone on November 18, 2026. Its strongest results from IFLScience and EarthSky align with that. B does contain the correct date in the snippet and top result, but it also presents multiple conflicting milestone dates (Nov 13, Nov 15, Nov 18), including within highly visible results, making it more likely an LLM would be confused or answer incorrectly.
